title: Phase 2 persistence layer: new tables and Alembic migration
description:
Add all Phase 2 SQLModel table classes and a new Alembic migration so Passes 4-9 have a stable schema to write to.

Tables to add (from PROPOSAL.md Section 12 / Phase 2 list):
- constraints (id, subject_id FK→entities, rule, constraint_type must/must_not, conditions JSON, exceptions JSON, + ProvenanceMixin columns)
- patterns (id, name, context JSON, problem, force_descriptions JSON, force_ids JSON, solution, consequences JSON, related_pattern_names JSON, related_pattern_ids JSON, emergent_properties JSON, + ProvenanceMixin columns)
- forces (id, name, direction increase/decrease, affects JSON entity UUIDs, + ProvenanceMixin columns)
- antipatterns (id, name, conditions JSON, consequences JSON, mitigations JSON, + ProvenanceMixin columns)
- process_relations (id, predecessor_id FK→entities, successor_id FK→entities, hard_constraint bool, rationale NOT NULL, + ProvenanceMixin columns; UniqueConstraint on (predecessor_id, successor_id, source_model) as per Section 4.6)
- spatial_relations (id, subject_id FK→entities, relation TEXT, object_id FK→entities, + ProvenanceMixin columns)
- pending_predicates (id, value, vocabulary_type='predicate', occurrence_count, first_seen_at, last_seen_at, flagged_for_review)
- pending_spatial_relation_types (same fields, vocabulary_type='spatial_relation')
- pending_force_refs (id, description TEXT, failure_type unresolved_ref/validation_failure, pattern_id FK optional, force_id FK optional, created_at)
- pending_pattern_refs (id, pattern_name TEXT, source_pattern_id FK→patterns, created_at)
- pending_entity_refs (id, entity_name TEXT, source_force_id FK→forces, created_at)
- predicate_mappings (id, from_predicate TEXT, to_predicate TEXT, created_at, reviewer TEXT)

Also add SPATIAL_RELATION_TYPES constant to bsos/vocab.py (Section 5.4: adjacent_to, contains, connects_to, accessible_from, above, below, enclosed_by) — already skeleton-tested in test_vocab.py.

Write Alembic migration (alembic/versions/) covering all tables above.
Add repository helper functions: upsert_pending_predicate(session, value, vocab_type), upsert_pending_spatial_relation_type(session, value).

title: Phase 2 MCP query tools: constraints, failure modes, patterns, forces, spatial, process sequence
description:
Extend the MCP server with all Phase 2 query tools (PROPOSAL.md Section 13.3).

New tools to add to bsos/mcp_server/server.py:

1. get_constraints(entity) → list[ConstraintResult]
   - Constraint records where subject_id matches entity (via resolve_entity)
   - Returns: rule, constraint_type, conditions, exceptions, confidence, knowledge_origin, status
   - Sort: confidence DESC then knowledge_origin priority (physical > engineering > architectural > cultural)

2. get_failure_modes(entity) → list[AntiPatternResult]
   - AntiPattern records where subject_id matches entity
   - Returns: name, conditions, consequences, mitigations, confidence, knowledge_origin, status

3. get_patterns(entity) → list[PatternResult]
   - Pattern records where subject_id matches entity
   - PatternResult.forces: populated from force_ids resolved to Force names; if force_ids empty and force_descriptions non-empty, use raw descriptions with a warning in the result
   - Returns: name, problem, solution, context, forces, consequences, emergent_properties, confidence, knowledge_origin, status

4. get_forces(entity) → list[ForceResult]
   - Force records where entity UUID appears in affects JSON array
   - Returns: name, direction, confidence, knowledge_origin, rationale, status

5. get_spatial_relations(entity) → list[SpatialRelationResult]
   - SpatialRelation records where subject_id OR object_id matches entity
   - Returns: subject (resolved name), relation, object (resolved name), confidence, knowledge_origin, status

6. get_process_sequence(entity, max_depth=50) → ProcessSequenceResult
   - Build local NetworkX DiGraph from ProcessRelation rows (predecessor→successor edges)
   - BFS/DFS from entity in both directions up to max_depth hops to find reachable subgraph
   - MUST call nx.is_directed_acyclic_graph; if cycle detected return has_cycle=True with partial sequence and cycle_description
   - If DAG: return nx.topological_sort result as sequence (entity names, predecessors first)
   - truncated=True if max_depth reached before exhausting graph

All tools MUST:
- Use resolve_entity (case-insensitive, alias-aware, skips merged)
- Return {'error': 'entity_not_found', ...} dict on unknown entity (not raise)
- Each create their own session (concurrency safe)
- Support min_confidence, max_results, include_proposed shared params (Section 13.1)

Update create_server() to register all new tools.
Tests: tests/integration/test_mcp_server_phase2.py covering each tool with fixture data; get_process_sequence DAG ordering test, cycle detection test.

title: Phase 2 CLI extensions: query types, status table, review-pending, validate topology/constraints
description:
Extend CLI commands to cover all Phase 2 knowledge types (PROPOSAL.md Sections 17-13 CLI specs).

1. bsos query — extend to support new --type values:
   - constraint, antipattern, force, spatial, process, abstraction (Section 17 query spec)
   - Route each type to corresponding get_* query function; format output per type
   - --min-confidence and --include-proposed already work; verify they apply to new types

2. bsos status — extend status table to include new item types (Section 17 status spec):
   - Add rows for: constraints, patterns, forces, antipatterns, process_relations, spatial_relations
   - Add summary lines: 'Pending predicates awaiting review: N', 'Pending spatial relation types awaiting review: N', 'Unresolved refs — force: N | pattern: N | entity: N'
   - Requires counts from: pending_predicates, pending_spatial_relation_types, pending_force_refs, pending_pattern_refs, pending_entity_refs tables

3. bsos review-pending — new subcommand (Section 17 review-pending spec):
   - Lists pending predicates and pending spatial relation types that have reached occurrence threshold
   - Threshold formula: min(50, round(5 + total_assertions * 0.005)) unless pending_predicate_threshold_override config key set
   - Display each pending item and prompt for decision: add-to-core / map-to <existing> / defer
   - Writes ReviewDecision record for each decision (but review_decisions table is Phase 3 — defer this part; for Phase 2, just print and allow mapping)
   - --type predicate|spatial-relation|force filter; --limit N; --stats mode
   - Phase 2 scope: implement predicate and spatial-relation review; force review is Phase 3

4. bsos validate --topology — check space entity reachability from entrance nodes (Section 17 validate spec):
   - Query Entity rows with entity_type='space'; query SpatialRelation rows with relation='accessible_from'
   - Build NetworkX DiGraph on accessible_from edges
   - Find entrance nodes: entities with is_entrance=True
   - If no entrance nodes: warn and skip
   - Report spaces not reachable from any entrance via graph path
   - bsos curate set-entrance <entity>: set is_entrance=True on named entity

5. bsos validate --constraints <entity> — LLM constraint checking (Section 17 validate spec):
   - Retrieve Constraint rows for entity; retrieve accepted assertions for entity
   - Submit LLM classification call per constraint
   - Schema: {violated: bool, violating_assertion_id: str|None, explanation: str|None}
   - Print violations; use constraint_validation_model config key if set

Tests: tests/integration/test_cli_phase2.py using subprocess + tmp db (or direct function calls)

title: Pass 8: Pattern Extraction
description:
Implement Pass 8: per-entity extraction of Alexander-style architectural patterns into Pattern records.

For each entity, ask: 'What architectural or spatial patterns involving X improve building quality? For each pattern, describe: the context where it applies, the problem it addresses, the competing forces at play, the solution, and what consequences follow from applying it.'

Implementation requirements (PROPOSAL.md Section 6, Pass 8 / Section 4.3):
- New extraction schema: PatternExtractionResponse with list[ExtractedPattern] (name, context, problem, force_descriptions, solution, consequences, emergent_properties, confidence, knowledge_origin)
- Note: at extraction time force_descriptions is free-text; force_ids starts empty. Pass 10a resolves force_descriptions → force_ids later.
- Also extract related_pattern_names as free-text; related_pattern_ids starts empty; Pass 10a resolves these.
- Write PatternRow with subject_id FK for the entity being processed; force_descriptions JSON; force_ids JSON (initially []); related_pattern_names JSON; related_pattern_ids JSON (initially [])
- Same parallelism model as Passes 3-7: run_pass8(engine, ...) ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4); resume semantics
- CLI: add Pass 8 block to bsos extract after Pass 7
- Tests: tests/integration/test_pass8.py covering writes pattern with force_descriptions, force_ids starts empty, related_pattern_names stored, resume semantics, dry-run

title: Pass 9: Force Extraction with direction validation
description:
Implement Pass 9: per-entity extraction of design pressures into Force records with direction consistency validation.

For each entity, ask: 'What individual design pressures act on decisions involving X? For each force: (1) force name as directional pressure (2) whether it increases or decreases its target quality (3) which entities it acts upon.'

Implementation requirements (PROPOSAL.md Section 6, Pass 9 / Section 4.4):
- New extraction schema: ForceExtractionResponse with list[ExtractedForce] (name, direction increase/decrease, affects list[str] entity names, confidence, knowledge_origin, rationale)
- ForceDirection consistency validation BEFORE storage: check name contains required qualifier for direction. increase → one of: increased, improved, enhanced, maximised, maximized, greater, higher, more, better, stronger, expanded, adequate, sufficient, optimised, optimized. decrease → one of: reduced, minimised, minimized, decreased, limited, lower, less, fewer, smaller, restricted, constrained, avoided, prevented, eliminated. Case-insensitive substring match.
- Validation failure: log via structlog WARNING, write to pending_force_refs with failure_type='validation_failure'; do NOT store the Force row
- Resolve affects entity names against entity table; unresolved names written to pending_entity_refs (not discarded)
- Extraction prompt MUST include the permitted qualifier terms (Section 6 Pass 9 exact wording)
- Same parallelism model as Passes 3-8: run_pass9(engine, ...) ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4); resume semantics
- CLI: add Pass 9 block to bsos extract after Pass 8
- Tests: tests/integration/test_pass9.py covering writes force, direction validation pass/fail, validation failure goes to pending_force_refs, unresolved affects written to pending_entity_refs, resume semantics

title: Pass 6: Constraint Extraction
description:
Implement Pass 6: per-entity extraction of hard binary rules into Constraint records.

For each entity, ask: 'What conditions MUST or MUST NOT hold for a valid building design involving X? List only binary constraints where violation makes the design physically invalid, unsafe, or inoperable — not typical functional dependencies or preferences.'

Implementation requirements (PROPOSAL.md Section 6, Pass 6 / Section 4.10):
- New extraction schema: ConstraintExtractionResponse with list[ExtractedConstraint] (rule, constraint_type must/must_not, conditions, exceptions, confidence, knowledge_origin, rationale)
- Prompt MUST include both calibration examples from Section 6 Pass 6:
  - Constraint example: 'roof must have a drainage path' (binary, no valid exception)
  - Assertion example: 'roof requires structural support' (dependency with form variation → use Assertion, not Constraint)
- Write ConstraintRow(subject_id=entity.id, rule, constraint_type, conditions JSON, exceptions JSON, + provenance)
- Same parallelism model as Pass 3-5: run_pass6(engine, ...) takes engine; ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4); resume semantics
- CLI: add Pass 6 block to bsos extract after Pass 5
- Tests: tests/integration/test_pass6.py covering writes constraint, correct constraint_type field, skips entity on empty response, resume semantics, dry-run

title: Pass 7: Anti-Pattern Extraction
description:
Implement Pass 7: per-entity extraction of failure conditions into AntiPattern records.

For each entity, ask: 'What are known failure conditions, pathological configurations, or design mistakes involving X that consistently lead to physical, functional, or performance failures?'

Implementation requirements (PROPOSAL.md Section 6, Pass 7 / Section 4.5):
- New extraction schema: AntiPatternExtractionResponse with list[ExtractedAntiPattern] (name, conditions, consequences, mitigations, confidence, knowledge_origin, rationale)
- Write AntiPatternRow(name, conditions JSON, consequences JSON, mitigations JSON, + ProvenanceMixin); AntiPattern is not entity-specific at the row level — link via a junction or store entity_id FK? Check PROPOSAL Section 4.5: no entity FK on AntiPattern. Pattern records are 'associated with entity' at query time (get_failure_modes queries by entity, but the AntiPattern schema has no subject_id). Need to add subject_id FK so get_failure_modes can query. If PROPOSAL is silent on FK: add subject_id FK to antipatterns table as the subject entity for which this anti-pattern was extracted.
- Same parallelism model as Passes 3-6: run_pass7(engine, ...) ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4); resume semantics
- CLI: add Pass 7 block to bsos extract after Pass 6
- Tests: tests/integration/test_pass7.py covering writes anti-pattern, conditions/consequences/mitigations stored as JSON, resume semantics, dry-run

title: Pass 5: Process/Sequence Extraction
description:
Implement Pass 5: per-entity extraction of temporal construction ordering into ProcessRelation records.

For each entity, ask the LLM: 'what must happen before X, and what can only happen after X?' Populate predecessor_id, successor_id, hard_constraint, and rationale (REQUIRED — model_validator enforces this).

Implementation requirements (PROPOSAL.md Section 6, Pass 5 / Section 4.6):
- New extraction schema: ProcessRelationExtractionResponse with list[ExtractedProcessRelation] (predecessor_name, successor_name, hard_constraint, rationale)
- Inline activity entity creation: when predecessor or successor names an activity not in the DB, create a new EntityRow(entity_type='activity', status='proposed') before inserting ProcessRelation; creation is idempotent (case-insensitive name check first); emit structlog WARNING each time an activity is created inline naming the activity and the entity being processed
- INSERT OR IGNORE deduplication keyed on (predecessor_id, successor_id, source_model) per UniqueConstraint; detect suppressed inserts by checking rowcount==0; SELECT existing row on suppression and compare hard_constraint; if they differ, emit structlog ERROR and add pair to in-memory list for summary
- Same parallelism model as Pass 3/4: run_pass5(engine, ...) takes engine; ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4)
- Resume semantics via pass_progress
- CLI: add Pass 5 block to bsos extract after Pass 4
- Tests: tests/integration/test_pass5.py covering writes process relation, inline activity creation (with WARNING), INSERT OR IGNORE dedup, hard_constraint divergence detection (ERROR log), resume semantics, rationale required validation

title: Pass 4: Spatial Relation Extraction
description:
Implement Pass 4: per-entity extraction of spatial and topological relationships into SpatialRelation records.

For each active (non-merged) entity, ask the LLM: 'what are the spatial and topological relationships between X and other building entities?' Map extracted relations to the SPATIAL_RELATION_TYPES controlled vocabulary. Unknown relation types are written to pending_spatial_relation_types rather than rejected.

Implementation requirements (PROPOSAL.md Section 6, Pass 4):
- New Pydantic extraction schema: SpatialRelationExtractionResponse with list[ExtractedSpatialRelation] (relation: str, object_name: str, confidence, knowledge_origin, rationale)
- Prompt template covering the SPATIAL_RELATION_TYPES vocabulary (adjacent_to, contains, connects_to, accessible_from, above, below, enclosed_by) so LLM selects from it; unknown types still accepted and written to pending_spatial_relation_types with occurrence-count upsert
- Same parallelism model as Pass 3: run_pass4(engine, provider, run_id, _embedder=None, max_workers=4) takes engine not session; each worker opens its own session; ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4)
- Resume semantics: check pass_progress before processing each entity (same pattern as Pass 3)
- Resolve object_name against entity names and aliases using _build_name_lookup; skip self-references; write pending_entity_refs for unresolved names (not reject)
- Write SpatialRelationRow; record pass_progress on entity completion
- CLI: add Pass 4 block to bsos extract (after Pass 3, same engine pattern)
- Tests: integration tests in tests/integration/test_pass4.py covering writes spatial relations, skips self-reference, writes unknown relation to pending_spatial_relation_types, resume semantics, dry-run; use fake embedder and FakeLLMProvider

title: Test suite: FakeLLMProvider, unit tests, and integration tests
description: Implement the three-level test suite from Section 16.7. FakeLLMProvider implementing LLMProvider protocol: dispatches on (type[BaseModel], entity_name) key, raises ValueError if entity_name is None, returns minimal empty response for unknown keys. Fixture dispatch table in tests/fixtures/fake_responses.py must include at least one duplicate pair, one contradictory pair, one process relation, and roof assertions (roof protects_from precipitation etc). Unit tests: Pydantic model validation, predicate registry lookup, threshold formula, entity merge algorithm. Integration tests: full single-model Passes 1-3 pipeline, graph node/edge counts, MCP server get_requirements call with concurrency safety check. All tests pass without network connection.
close_reason: Standard fixture factory with duplicate/contradictory/process pairs added; vocab unit tests for predicate registry, thresholds; full Passes 1-3 E2E pipeline test with MCP concurrency safety — 32 new tests, 132 total passing

title: MCP server with get_requirements and get_dependencies tools
description: Implement the MCP server (Section 13) using the mcp Python SDK with stdio transport. Register get_requirements(entity) returning list[AssertionResult] for assertions with predicate in {requires, depends_on} where subject matches entity. Register get_dependencies(entity) returning list[AssertionResult] for assertions where predicate is depends_on and either subject or object matches entity. Entity resolution is case-insensitive against Entity.name and entity_aliases. Each tool call builds its own subgraph (concurrency safety, Section 13.1). Entity-not-found returns structured error dict, not exception. bsos serve command with --transport stdio|sse flags. Requires at least one entity in DB on startup.
close_reason: MCP server implemented with get_requirements and get_dependencies tools, bsos serve CLI with --transport stdio|sse, entity-not-found error dicts, 17 tests passing

title: bsos status and bsos query --type assertion CLI commands
description: Implement bsos status (Section 17 status spec) and bsos query with --type assertion support (Section 17 query spec). status displays the item count table (proposed/accepted/conflicted/deprecated totals per type) plus passes completed, pending predicates count, LLM cache size, conflict queue size. For Phase 1 only entities and assertions rows are populated. query resolves the entity name against Entity.name and entity_aliases (case-insensitive), returns AssertionResult records sorted by confidence DESC then knowledge_origin priority then created_at ASC. --json flag outputs JSON. Entity-not-found exits with code 1.
close_reason: bsos status (table + JSON output) and bsos query --type assertion (confidence sort, case-insensitive entity lookup, --include-proposed, --json, --min-confidence) implemented. 9 integration tests, 50 total passing.

title: Pass 3: Relationship Extraction with cross-prompt consistency
description: Implement Pass 3 from Section 6. For each entity, runs minimum 3 distinct prompt framings to extract Assertion records (subject_id, predicate, object_id with cached entity types, conditions, exceptions, applicability). Computes cross_prompt_consistency using: embed all assertion texts into temporary NumPy array, linear_sum_assignment (scipy) for bijective cross-framing matching (cosine >= 0.70 threshold), mean pairwise similarity of aligned groups stored on canonical assertion. Stores prompt_framing_count. Implements pass_progress resume semantics (skip completed entities). Implements 4-worker global ThreadPoolExecutor for entity-level parallelism with sequential per-entity pass ordering.
close_reason: Pass 3 implemented: 3 prompt framings, LAP-based cross-prompt consistency, resume semantics, 4-worker ThreadPoolExecutor, 12 tests passing

title: Pass 2: Entity Deduplication with embedding-based clustering
description: Implement Pass 2 from Section 6 / Sections 8.1-8.2. Computes all-mpnet-base-v2 embeddings for all entity names. Runs AgglomerativeClustering (cosine metric, average linkage, distance_threshold=0.20). For clusters of size >= 2, runs canonical concept formation: elect canonical entity (most assertions, then earliest created_at), insert aliases into entity_aliases, UPDATE all FK references in assertions/spatial_relations/process_relations/constraints/forces.affects (handling JSON-encoded lists in Python). All in one transaction per merge. Implements the sentence-transformer download confirmation flow (embedding_model_confirmed config key, Section 2). Must complete before Passes 3-9.
close_reason: Pass 2 implemented with embedding cache, cosine clustering, canonical election, FK re-pointing, and 11 integration tests all passing

title: Pass 1: Concept Discovery extraction pipeline
description: Implement Pass 1 from Section 6. Accepts a seed topic (user-supplied concept list, free-text description, or default bootstrap). Calls LLMProvider to enumerate building concepts including construction activities. Expands each top-level concept for sub-concepts before committing. Writes Entity records to the entities table with source_model, source_prompt, created_at. Logs an extraction_runs row. Implements the bsos.lock file-level concurrency lock (Section 6.2) preventing concurrent bsos extract processes. Also implements the --seed and --models flags for bsos extract (Section 17 extract spec).
close_reason: Pass 1 pipeline implemented and all 10 integration tests passing

title: bsos init and bsos config CLI commands
description: Implement the init and config CLI commands (Section 17 init spec and config spec). init creates the SQLite database, runs alembic upgrade head, creates the abstraction_node_effective_origins view, writes .bsos_config and appends to .gitignore. config supports get/set/unset/list subcommands backed by the config SQLite table. Database path resolution uses the three-step lookup: --db flag, BSOS_DB env var, .bsos_config file. All documented config keys from Section 17 should be handled.
close_reason: bsos init and bsos config implemented. DB path resolution (--db / BSOS_DB / .bsos_config), alembic stamp, views creation, default config keys, .gitignore append. 10 integration tests, 41 total passing.

title: LLMProvider protocol + OpenAI-compatible instructor implementation
description: Implement the LLMProvider protocol (Section 2) with extract(), classify(), and model_id members. Implement OpenAIProvider concrete class using instructor for structured output. Include LLM API resilience: exponential backoff with jitter (3 attempts, 1s initial, 2x multiplier, 60s max), configurable timeout (llm_timeout_seconds from config), rate limit header respect, non-retryable error handling (401/403/400 exits immediately). Also implement the LLM response cache (llm_response_cache table, keyed on (model, SHA-256 prompt_hash)) from Section 6.
close_reason: LLMProvider protocol, OpenAIProvider with instructor, retry/backoff helper, LLM response cache implemented. 13 unit tests passing.

title: Project scaffold: pyproject.toml, src/bsos layout, CLI entry point
description: Set up the bsos Python project with src/ layout, pyproject.toml (hatchling build backend), all required dependencies listed in Section 2, and the bsos CLI entry point via Typer. Create the directory structure from Section 2.5: src/bsos/__init__.py, cli/, models/, persistence/, pipeline/, llm/, vocab.py, config.py. Also alembic/ directory with env.py and alembic.ini. This is the prerequisite scaffold for all other Phase 1 work.
close_reason: Scaffold complete: pyproject.toml, src/bsos layout, CLI entry point, alembic/, tests/ all in place. bsos --help verified.

title: Core Pydantic extraction models: ProvenanceMixin, Entity, Assertion
description: Implement the core Pydantic data models from Section 4: BaseProvenanceMixin, ProvenanceMixin, Entity (Section 4.1), and Assertion (Section 4.2). Also implement CORE_PREDICATES frozenset, PREDICATE_REGISTRY dict with PredicateDefinition, and SPATIAL_RELATION_TYPES list from Sections 5.1-5.4 in src/bsos/vocab.py. These models are the foundation for the extraction pipeline and persistence layer.
close_reason: All Section 4 Pydantic models implemented in src/bsos/models/. 10 unit tests passing. Vocab already complete from scaffold.

title: SQLModel persistence layer + Alembic migrations for Phase 1 tables
description: Implement the SQLModel (table=True) persistence classes mirroring Phase 1 Pydantic models. Tables needed: entities, entity_aliases, assertions, embeddings, pass_progress, llm_response_cache, extraction_runs, config. Create the database engine with WAL mode (PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL, Section 12), session factory, BaseRepository with to_persistence/from_persistence, and Alembic migration for the initial schema. The abstraction_node_effective_origins view must be created by bsos init (Section 4.8), not Alembic.
close_reason: SQLModel persistence models, WAL-mode engine, BaseRepository, EntityRepository, AssertionRepository, Alembic initial migration, abstraction_node_effective_origins view. 8 integration + 31 total tests passing.

title: Ollama / local model LLMProvider implementation
description:
Add a concrete LLMProvider implementation for local models via Ollama (PROPOSAL.md Section 2, Phase 2 requirement).

The LLMProvider protocol is already defined (extract() and classify() methods). Phase 1 has an OpenAI-compatible provider. Phase 2 requires an Ollama fallback path.

Implementation:
- OllamaProvider class in bsos/providers/ollama.py implementing LLMProvider protocol
- Uses ollama Python client (or direct HTTP to localhost:11434)
- Structured output: use instructor with ollama backend, or use Ollama's native JSON mode if instructor doesn't support it
- extract(prompt, schema, *, entity_name) → BaseModel: POST to Ollama with schema as JSON mode constraint
- classify(prompt, options) → str: simple prompt to select from options list
- model_id property returns the Ollama model name (e.g. 'llama3.2', 'mistral')
- Retry logic: wrap all calls with exponential backoff (3 attempts, 1s initial, 2.0x, max 60s) per Section 6.2
- Timeout: respect llm_timeout_seconds config key (default 120s)
- CLI integration: bsos extract --models ollama/llama3.2 should route to OllamaProvider (detect 'ollama/' prefix in model id, or use 'ollama' as provider keyword)
- Config: document how to set default_llm_model to an Ollama model

Tests: unit tests with mock HTTP responses (or skip if Ollama not running — mark with pytest.mark.requires_ollama); at minimum test that OllamaProvider implements the LLMProvider protocol and that retry logic fires on simulated failure.
